job_description:
• Support process transfer to production facilities (some domestic and/or international travel may be required).
• Developing dry etch processes to meet the physical & electrical requirements of Micron’s products.
• Working knowledge in statistics, preferably in statistical process control.
• Education: BS + 2 years of relevant semiconductor experience (required) MS / PhD in a related field specific to semiconductor R&D (preferred) Ability to multi-task and manage numerous projects simultaneously.
• Academic or basic knowledge of one of the following: thermodynamics, transport phenomena, dry etch processing, kinetic theory, or basic plasma physics.
• Optimizing dry etch processes to reduce cost, process variability, and improve process capability.
• Ability to work independently, with minimal direction, and a focus on meeting commitments.
• Ability to resolve complex issues through root-cause or model-based problem solving.

job_title: Software Engineer, Kernels and Performance, Core ML Frameworks
